included.description:
Strategic HR Leadership Responsibilities

- Spearhead initiatives to enhance organizational effectiveness by developing and implementing comprehensive people operations strategies aligned with the company’s growth objectives.
- Oversee and streamline all HR functions, ensuring both tactical execution and strategic alignment with organizational goals, while reporting directly to the CEO.
- Collaborate with executive leadership to identify and address organizational capability needs, formulating targeted development plans, recruitment strategies, and retention programs to build a high-performing workforce.
- Partner with Department, Regional, and Clinic Managers to resolve HR challenges and drive a culture focused on performance and accountability.
- Act as a change agent by proactively assessing strategic drivers of change and crafting execution plans to support the organization’s expansion initiatives.
- Lead the integration of HR practices for onboarding new employees from acquired businesses, ensuring a seamless transition and alignment with company culture.
- Conduct thorough analyses to identify competency and talent gaps, designing and implementing tailored solutions to enhance leadership capabilities across the organization.
- Manage the coordination and execution of company-wide performance reviews and compensation adjustments to promote employee engagement and retention.
- Stay abreast of legal compliance and regulatory requirements impacting HR functions, ensuring that all policies and procedures adhere to relevant legislation.
- Oversee external HR partnerships and Professional Employer Organization (PEO) relationships to optimize compliance, training, benefits, and other essential HR programs.
included.title: Human Resources Manager

included.description:
•	Responsible for the recruitment and placement of all talent, from entry level positions to senior management.
•	Created New Hire Orientation Presentation to include: company history, executive bios, uniform policy, timesheet procedures, code of conduct, holiday schedule, company resources, and other HR information
•	Trained all managers and supervisors on interviewing techniques and best practices
•	Administered all Worker’s Compensation and FMLA Cases
•	Training all payroll staff on procedures, overtime calculations, Prevailing Wage, Living Wage and other wage ordinances.
•	Managed personnel records, and administered benefits enrollment and programs
•	Representing the company in labor hearings
•	Responsible for Certified Payroll
•                  Employee Benefits Administration including enrollments, terminations, and changes;
•                 Conflict resolution with employees - re: issues, problems, questions
•                 Monitor compliance of various HR-related issues (Employee Handbook, OSHA, Federal Postings, etc.)
included.title: Human Resources Generalist

included.description:
REGIONS: Northern California, Oregon, and Washington 

•Integral part of the roll out, reinforcement, and follow-up of company Recruitment and Retention Program
• Helped attain company goal of reducing turnover from 102% to 50% 
• Analyze branch turn over and trends to forecast appropriate recruitment activities
•Train hiring managers on interviewing techniques and guidelines 
• Develop recruitment/marketing strategies to peak candidate interest
• Utilize online recruiting resources, review resumes and credentials for appropriateness of skills, experience and knowledge in relation to position requirements. Screen potential candidates, coordinate/conduct interview, and on-board new hires
• Recruit, screen, evaluate and match qualified candidates to branch needs
• Organize, plan, and participate in college career fairs and job fairs to recruit top talent
included.title: Regional Human Resources Business Partner

included.description:
Human Resources Manager for Northern California Division 
LOCATIONS: San Jose, Concord, Santa Rosa, and San Bruno

Responsibilities include but not limited to:

 - Working collaboratively with managers and clinical leadership to provide coaching and advice in all areas of employee relations, performance management, and policy interpretation.

 - Conducting all internal investigations and making recommendations for appropriate actions.

 - Creating and administering all documentation including but not limited to corrective action, performance, and termination.

 - Conducting new hire training and all on-boarding functions.

 - Performing all Benefit, Payroll, Worker's Compensation, EDD, and LOA/FMLA administration.

 - Identifying legal and compliance risks within the organization and collaborating with legal counsel to mitigate these risks.
included.title: Employee Relations Consultant

multiLocaleSummary.en_US: As Director of Human Resources at JumpstartMD, I partner with executive leadership to align people operations with business performance. My focus is building compliant, accountable, and high-performing teams in complex, member-facing healthcare environments.I specialize in:• Strategic workforce planning• Full-cycle recruiting optimization• Multi-site operations support• HR compliance & risk mitigation (California-focused)• Leadership coaching & performance management• Process development & infrastructure scalingWith over 10 years in human resources. I am committed to empowering teams, driving retention initiatives, and implementing solutions that promote organizational effectiveness.

multiLocaleHeadline.en_US: Senior Director of Human Resources | Strategic HR Leadership
